changes_summary: Addressed both significant flags. FLAG-001: added a new Step 2 that wires `onLostPointerCapture` on TimelineCanvas resize handles, routes through `endResize()` + `notifyInteractionEndIfIdle()`, and adds a regression test — directly tied to the "doesn't release frames after release" user symptom. FLAG-002: resolved the acceptance-command ambiguity by specifying exact gate commands in the Overview (filtered `tsc -p tsconfig.app.json --noEmit` over `src/tools/video-editor/` lines, plus `vitest run src/tools/video-editor`) and reused them in Step 9. Renumbered downstream steps. Tightened `imageClipSnapshot` to store absolute bounds (resolves an open metadata question). No scope creep — all changes stay inside `src/tools/video-editor`.
questions: If the `onLostPointerCapture` fallback fires while a grouped commit is mid-flight (e.g., `applyEdit` already queued), should it be a no-op, or should it still flush autosave once the in-flight commit settles?

success_criteria.criterion: `rowsToConfig()` commits preserve grouped clip adjacency across repeated interior-boundary resizes, with regression coverage proving no gap/overlap drift and no boundary jump into the neighboring clip.

Resize handles in TimelineCanvas recover from lost pointer capture: an `onLostPointerCapture` path tears down the resize session, clears `resizeOverrides`, and notifies interaction-end so autosave/poll-sync flush. Regression test fires `lostpointercapture` mid-resize and asserts cleanup.
`repairConfig()` converts legacy projection-shaped `pinnedShotGroups` into soft-tag groups with `clipIds` only, and migration coverage passes.

Grouped cross-track moves, final-video switching, image restore, and shot drops commit through soft-tag row/meta/clip-order/group overrides rather than legacy shim-only updates, and the affected hook tests pass.
Scoped TypeScript gate produces zero lines starting with `src/tools/video-editor/` when running `npx tsc -p tsconfig.app.json --noEmit` and filtering output.

`PinnedShotGroup.start`, `PinnedShotGroup.children`, and the five deprecated shim exports (`buildGroupFromChildren`, `buildGroupFromConfigClips`, `replaceGroupChildren`, `moveGroup`, `projectPinnedShotGroups`) are physically deleted with no remaining imports/usages in `src/tools/video-editor`.
No diagnostic `console.log` calls remain for `[handleResizePointerDown]`, `[outer-edge lookup]`, `[GroupEdgeResize]`, `[startGroupEdgeResize]`, `[useClipDrag]`, or `[Marquee]`.
Shot-group-related files end with a net lower line count after compatibility code is removed.
Manual smoke of the reported symptoms and the listed end-to-end flows confirms boundary releases are stable and preview frames clear correctly after release (including lost-capture cases).

assumptions: The existing `translateGroupMembers()` helper in `useTimelineTrackManagement.ts` is the intended replacement for the three `moveGroup()` callsites, and extending it is preferable to adding new state flow.
A shared `4`-decimal canonical rounding policy is acceptable for video-editor commits and is sufficient to stop interior-boundary drift without reworking `applyEdit()` or `materializeData()`.

`PinnedShotImageClipSnapshot` may gain explicit absolute-bounds timing data (`at`/`end`/`from`/`to`/`trackId`/`rowIndex`) because it is editor-owned pinned-group metadata, not an external public contract.

Filtering full-project `tsc -p tsconfig.app.json --noEmit` output to lines under `src/tools/video-editor/` is an acceptable substitute for a video-editor-only tsconfig, which the repo does not have and is out of scope to add.
Unrelated repo-wide TypeScript or test failures remain out of scope; acceptance is limited to regressions introduced in `src/tools/video-editor`.
No production code outside `src/tools/video-editor` depends on the five deprecated shim exports or on `PinnedShotGroup.start` / `PinnedShotGroup.children`.

Routing the lost-pointer-capture handler through the existing `endResize()` path is sufficient — no separate state machine is needed because the unified resize session already handles all three resize variants.
